title: entada phaseoloides subsp. tonkinensis
intro: Entada phaseoloides subsp. tonkinensis, also known as the Tonkin Entada or Tonkin coral creeper, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia. It is a climber with fragrant flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Entada phaseoloides subsp. tonkinensis include the potential treatment of various diseases such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems are used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that are believed to promote overall health and well-being.
